interrogator_dossier:
# Interrogator Forensic Dossier — Boyd Gaming Corporation (BYD)

> Note on identity: this is **Boyd Gaming** (NYSE: BYD), a US regional/locals casino operator — not the Chinese EV maker. All transcript evidence (Keith Smith, Josh Hirsberg, Las Vegas Locals / Downtown / Midwest & South / Online / Managed segments, the FanDuel stake) confirms this. The "Casino 78% of revenue" segment block is Boyd's gaming revenue, not vehicles.

## 1. NARRATIVE ARC

Across six consecutive calls (Q1'25 through Q1'26), the Boyd story is one of **steady, defensible, low-drama compounding under a single deliberate strategic shift**: monetizing a passive financial asset to fortify an already strong balance sheet.

- **Opening emphasis (Q1'25):** "consistent results," EBITDAR $338M, property margins 40%, the diversified-segment resilience pitch, and an explicit nod to the **5% FanDuel equity stake** as "significant and growing value." Management leaned on the locals/core-customer durability story while flagging early-2025 macro uncertainty (tariffs, "increased level of economic uncertainty").
- **The pivot (Q2'25):** The single most important event in the window — Boyd announced the **sale of its 5% FanDuel stake to Flutter for $1.755B cash**, simultaneously extending market-access agreements to 2038 and re-pricing them downward. The narrative shifts from "we own a growing call option on online gaming" to "we have crystallized that value and will use it to de-lever below 2x." This is a clean, value-realizing, one-time event — and management framed it correctly as such.
- **Mid-window (Q3'25–Q4'25):** Story bifurcates into (a) **core operating durability** — locals core-customer play "continued its long-term growth trend," Midwest & South posting "strongest Q3 in 3 years" — and (b) a **growing destination-business drag**, centered on the Orleans and the IP Biloxi, plus the Las Vegas Strip softness bleeding into downtown pedestrian traffic (Fremont Street -10% in Q4). Leverage hits 1.5x; capital-return becomes the headline (>$800M returned in 2025, share count -11%).
- **Current (Q1'26):** The narrative is now **defensive-operational**: "broad-based strength in Midwest & South, partially offset by softer destination business and Suncoast construction disruption." Online EBITDAR guided DOWN to $30–35M for 2026 (from $63M in 2025) — the direct mechanical consequence of selling FanDuel and re-pricing market access. Growth drivers introduced/sustained: Cadence Crossing (opened Mar 2026), Suncoast modernization (completes Q3'26), the $750M Norfolk Virginia resort (late-2027), Par-A-Dice rebuild (2028), Sky River managed-fee growth.

**Drivers quietly dropped/diminished:** the FanDuel "growing value" optionality (sold); the hope that Locals could "eke out growth" in 2026 (Q1'26 Q&A effectively walked this back — destination + Suncoast drag). **Drivers added:** tax-legislation tailwind to consumer discretionary income (repeated heavily, unverified), accelerated-depreciation cash-tax benefit (~$45–50M timing benefit in 2026).

## 2. CLAIMS vs FINANCIALS (forensic)

| Mgmt claim | Transcript evidence | What the financials say | Verdict |
|---|---|---|---|
| "Record company-wide revenues," steady growth | Q4'25: "achieved record company-wide revenues" | Rev 3.37B→3.56B→3.74B→3.93B→4.09B; +4.1% YoY, 3-yr CAGR +4.8% | **MATCHES** — low-single-digit top line, genuinely steady, no inflection |
| EPS +264.5% YoY / "delivering value" | EPS 6.19→22.56 | TRUE but ~$1.4B of it is the **one-time after-tax FanDuel gain** | **MISLEADING optic** — normalized EPS ~ low-$6 range; the screener's 420 "Buffett fair value" is built on this distorted EPS and is invalid |
| Strong FCF / capital return | ">$800M returned in 2025, share count -11%" | FY FCF margin 9.5% but **FCF -30.2% YoY**, 3-yr CAGR -18.1%; **TTM FCF only 281M** | **DIVERGES** — operating FCF is shrinking; the $800M return was funded substantially by the FanDuel cash, not recurring FCF |
| "Strongest balance sheet in our history," leverage to 1.5x | Q3'25/Q4'25 | LIVE correction: **NET DEBT 2.55B** (debt 2.92B – cash 373M). Traditional leverage low, but lease-adjusted ~2.7–2.9x (mgmt: "just under 3x") | **PARTIALLY** — gross leverage genuinely low post-FanDuel, but this is a **net-debt liability, not net cash**; lease-adjusted leverage is mid-pack, not pristine |
| Margins consistent at ~40% | "property-level margins 40%, consistent" | Gross 42.1% (contracting), company EBITDAR margin drifting 40%→37%→39% | **MATCHES with erosion** — margins holding but the trend label "contracting" on gross is real |
| Core customer "most consistent," resilient | Repeated every quarter | Revenue genuinely steady; no quarter of core-customer decline claimed | **CREDIBLE** — corroborated by flat-to-up regional GGR |
| Online segment a growth pillar | Early-2025 framing | Online EBITDAR **$63M (2025) → $30–35M guide (2026)** | **REVERSES** — selling FanDuel structurally halved this segment's profit |

**Net read:** the *operating* business does roughly what management says — steady ~4% top line, durable locals core, holding margins — but the *headline* financials (EPS +265%, the 381% MoS, 420 fair value) are an artifact of a one-time gain and must be stripped out. The story is **poorer than the trajectory optics** but **as advertised on the underlying operation**.

## 3. TONE & CONFIDENCE TRAJECTORY

**Rating: STABLE, drifting mildly defensive.**

- **Q1'25 (cautious-confident):** "we recognize that the last several weeks have brought an increased level of economic uncertainty" balanced by "strongest balance sheet in our history."
- **Q2'25 (most confident):** "property level year-over-year revenue and EBITDAR growth was the strongest in more than 3 years"; FanDuel sale framed as "unlocking the significant value." Peak offensive tone.
- **Q3'25–Q4'25 (measured):** increasing airtime on "softness in destination business," "lower hotel revenues," Fremont -10%. Still growth-framed but the hedging language grows.
- **Q1'26 (defensive-operational):** the language is now dominated by *mitigation* — "construction disruption," "softer destination business," walking back Locals growth hopes ("we don't typically provide guidance… enough information out there to figure it out"). Josh Hirsberg's COVID-era "no concept of fixed costs beyond property taxes, insurance, rent and interest — everything becomes variable" is classic **defensive efficiency** framing.

Guidance specificity *increased* over time (they gave hard Online and Managed EBITDAR numbers for 2026, and quantified Suncoast/weather drags to the $M) — a credibility positive, not a red flag.

## 4. GUIDANCE CREDIBILITY

**Reliability: HIGH.** Boyd is a low-promise/reliable-delivery operator with an 86% beat rate (6/7 qtrs).

- FanDuel: guided "close and receive proceeds in the next several weeks" (Q2'25) → delivered, leverage to 1.5x by Q3'25. **Delivered exactly.**
- Online: guided up to $60M for 2025 (Q3'25) → delivered $63M (Q4'25). **Slight over-delivery.** Then honestly guided DOWN to $30–35M for 2026 — no sandbagging, no spin.
- Suncoast disruption: in mid-2025 management floated that disruption might be minimal; in Q1'26 they candidly reversed — "we said we'll let you know when we see it… and so we're seeing it, and we're letting you know." That intellectual honesty is a credibility green flag.
- Weather: quantified ~$5M Q1 impact in both 2025 and 2026, consistently.

Pattern is **mild conservatism + candid bad-news delivery**, not promotional. The only "miss" is the Locals-growth hope that Q1'26 walked back — and they pre-flagged it rather than burying it.

## 5. ANALYST PRESSURE POINTS

- **Destination / Strip softness (recurring, escalating):** analysts (Beynon, Hargreaves, Bender, Hardiman) pressed every quarter on whether Strip weakness bleeds into Locals/Downtown. Management consistently quarantined it to "the Orleans and the IP Biloxi," but the drag widened across the window — the topic analysts pushed on *did* turn into the persistent headwind.
- **Capital allocation post-FanDuel (Stephen Grambling, Q3'25):** "leverage pushed to 1.5… what will you do with it?" Management deflected — "we don't have an answer for you right now… could be M&A, could tick back up." **Unresolved $1B+ capital-deployment question** is the single biggest open item.
- **M&A discipline (Q1'26):** analysts probed underwriting synergies; Keith conceded "sellers now have a very high expectation of getting a part of those synergies." This signals a **rich M&A market where Boyd may overpay** if it deploys the dry powder — a genuine risk given the prior Penn Entertainment approach.
- **Suncoast quantification (Hardiman, Q1'26):** analyst forced management to size the drag ($1.5M Q1, ~$2.5–3M Q2/Q3) and effectively extract that Locals won't grow in 2026.

## 6. RED FLAGS / GREEN FLAGS

🔴 **Online segment profit structurally halved** ($63M→$30–35M) — the FanDuel sale removed a growth pillar; this is permanent.
🔴 **Operating FCF shrinking** (FCF -30% YoY, TTM FCF only $281M) against a $2.55B net-debt position and a heavy multi-year capex pipeline (Norfolk $750M, Par-A-Dice, Suncoast, Cadence) — the "fortress balance sheet" is funding an elevated growth-capex cycle.
🔴 **Destination/Strip drag widening** — Fremont -10% (Q4'25) to -11% (Q1'26); not yet contained.
🔴 **$1B+ undeployed capital + stated appetite for "larger transactions"** in a seller's M&A market = overpayment risk (the Penn approach precedent).
🔴 **Insider open-market activity: 0 buys vs 24 sells (802,022 sh)** — no conviction buying at $87 near the 52-wk high.

🟢 **Genuine value realization** — crystallized $1.755B from a passive 5% stake at a premium; textbook good capital allocation.
🟢 **Aggressive, accretive buyback** — share count -11% in one year at reasonable prices.
🟢 **Low gross leverage post-FanDuel** (~1.5x traditional) — real flexibility.
🟢 **Durable core-customer/locals franchise** — no quarter of core decline; structural Southern Nevada population/wage tailwind (pop +16%/decade, wages +5–6%).
🟢 **Candid, reliable management** — pre-flags bad news, delivers guidance, no promotional spin.

## 7. HIDDEN SIGNALS

- **Terminology shift from "value" to "mitigation":** early calls talk about "unlocking value" and "growth opportunities"; the latest call is dominated by "managing through," "disruption," "softer." The center of gravity moved from offense to defense even as the headline EPS exploded (on the one-time gain).
- **Capital-allocation ambiguity is deliberate:** "could be M&A, could be other things, could tick back up" — management is keeping optionality on $1B+, which the market is not pricing as either a buyback floor or an M&A premium. This is the live uncertainty.
- **Market-access re-pricing buried in the FanDuel deal:** they "adjusted our market access rates" — i.e., gave Flutter a price concession to extend to 2038. The 2026 Online guide-down is partly this, not just the lost equity income. Management discloses it but doesn't dwell.
- **Tax-legislation tailwind invoked repeatedly** (tips/overtime/senior deductions) as a forward consumer-spending driver — an unverifiable, convenient macro crutch leaned on across three calls.
- **Asset pruning:** Tunica and Shreveport closed/sold ("scraped clean," "raw land"); management says no more to come. Minor housekeeping, signals portfolio discipline.

## 8. CAPITAL-ALLOCATION VERDICT (for the director)

**Classification: STABLE.** Boyd is a well-run, candid, steadily-compounding regional casino operator that just executed one excellent, **one-time** value-realization (FanDuel) and returned the proceeds via de-leveraging and a large buyback. The underlying operating business grows low-single-digits with durable but not accelerating economics and a real, widening destination-business drag.

**The single most important thing the director must weigh before sizing:** the entire "deep value / 381% margin of safety / $420 fair value" signal is a **screener artifact** — it is built on 2025 EPS of $22.56 that contains ~$1.4B of one-time FanDuel gain. Normalized, Boyd is a ~13–18x P/FCF regional casino trading near its 52-week high at ~$87 with a *spent* catalyst, shrinking operating FCF, a $2.55B net-debt liability, and $1B+ of undeployed capital that management may spend on an expensive acquisition. There is no live forcing function and no margin of safety on normalized numbers. Do NOT size this as a deep-value special-situation; the value lever is fairly-priced-to-mildly-rich, and the catalyst has already fired.

CREDIBILITY_SCORE: 4 | TRAJECTORY: STABLE

interrogator_dossier:
# Boyd Gaming Corporation (BYD) — Forensic Dossier

*Interrogator: Claude Opus 4.8 · Investment Committee · 2026-06-17*
*Source: four consecutive quarterly transcripts (Q1-2025 through Q4/FY-2025, calls dated 2025-04-24, 2025-07-24, 2025-10-23, 2026-02-05) cross-referenced against the screener financial trajectory.*

> **Identity check first.** This is **Boyd Gaming Corporation**, the US regional casino operator (NYSE: BYD) — *not* BYD Company, the Chinese EV manufacturer. The transcripts (Keith Smith / Josh Hirsberg, "Boyd Gaming First Quarter Conference Call"), the segment block (Casino 78%, Food & Beverage, Occupancy, Management Fee) and the peer set (MGM/CZR/PENN/Churchill) all confirm it unambiguously. Any "EV/autonomous-mobility" framing would be a category error.

---

## 1. NARRATIVE ARC

The story across the four calls is **remarkably consistent and low-drama** — by design. Boyd's entire pitch is *"diversified, disciplined, consistent."* The one genuine plot point is the **FanDuel monetization**.

- **Q1-2025 (Apr):** Pure steady-state. Keith Smith opens with "continued to deliver consistent results," EBITDA $338M, property margins 40% "consistent with the prior year." The FanDuel 5% stake is mentioned as embedded optionality — "represents significant and growing value for our shareholders." Macro caution is introduced ("increased level of economic uncertainty") but immediately neutralized by balance-sheet bragging ("strongest balance sheet in our history").
- **Q2-2025 (Jul):** The **pivotal call.** Smith leads — before results — with the FanDuel sale: "we reached an agreement to sell our 5% equity interest in FanDuel to Flutter Entertainment for $1.755 billion in cash, unlocking the significant value." Proceeds earmarked to "pay down debt, reducing our leverage below 2x." Market-access agreements extended to 2038 (but at *adjusted rates* — a quiet give-back). Operationally the strongest call: "year-over-year revenue and EBITDAR growth was the strongest in more than 3 years."
- **Q3-2025 (Oct):** Post-monetization. Leverage now **1.5x**. The narrative pivots subtly from "unlock value" to "what do we do with the firepower" — Smith: "could be M&A, could be other things, could tick back up... thoughtful discussions." First real cracks in the *destination/Strip* business surface (Orleans softness, Downtown pedestrian traffic down). Online segment guidance *cut* to $30M for 2026 (from the FanDuel rate reset).
- **Q4/FY-2025 (Feb-2026):** Capital-return victory lap — "$800M returned to shareholders... reducing total share count by 11%." 2026 setup is all **self-help capital projects** (Cadence Crossing, Suncoast completion, Ameristar convention center) plus a **macro hope** (the tax bill's tips/overtime/senior deductions "will benefit consumer spending"). Destination-business weakness reframed as isolated to "the IP, not a broader issue."

**Drivers introduced:** FanDuel cash, M&A optionality, tax-bill consumer tailwind, growth-capex pipeline.
**Drivers quietly dropped/faded:** the FanDuel "growing value" story (now sold/spent); the Online segment is now a *shrinking* contributor ($63M FY25 → $30-35M 2026 guide).

---

## 2. CLAIMS vs FINANCIALS (forensic)

The single most important forensic finding: **the screener's headline valuation is corrupted by a one-time gain.** The FY-2025 figures — Net income $1.84B, EPS $22.56 (+264.5% YoY), Net margin 45.0% — are inflated by the **~$1.4–1.8B pre-/after-tax FanDuel disposal gain** booked in 2025. This is *not* recurring earnings power. Operating EBITDAR was ~$1.4B, **flat** YoY ("consistent with last year").

| Mgmt claim | Transcript quote | Hard financials | Verdict |
|---|---|---|---|
| "Record company-wide revenues" / consistent growth | Q4: "achieved record company-wide revenues" | Rev $4.09B, **+4.1% YoY**, 3-yr CAGR +4.8% | **TRUE but pedestrian** — low-single-digit, GDP-like growth |
| Earnings exploded (+264% EPS) | (implied by FY result) | EPS $22.56 vs $6.19; **but TTM diluted EPS $22.81 and latest-Q EPS YoY only +5%** | **MISLEADING** — the jump is the FanDuel gain, not operations. Underlying Q EPS growth is ~+5% |
| Margins "consistent," 40% property-level | Q1/Q2/Q4: "property-level margins of 40%... consistent with the prior year" | Gross 42.1% **(contracting)**, EBITDAR flat | **TRUE** — margins are stable-to-softening, *not* the 45% net the screener shows |
| Strong FCF / capital returns | Q4: "robust free cash flow... returned more than $800M" | **TTM FCF only $281M**; FCF growth **−30.2% YoY**, 3-yr CAGR **−18.1%**; P/FCF 18.2x | **DIVERGES SHARPLY** — the $800M return was funded substantially by the FanDuel proceeds + balance sheet, *not* by $281M of TTM organic FCF. Growth-capex ($100M/yr recurring + Norfolk $750M + Suncoast) is eating FCF |
| "Strongest balance sheet in our history," delevered | Q2/Q3: "leverage below 2x"... "1.5x" | **Live net debt $2.55B** (total debt $2.92B − cash $373M); Altman-Z 3.3 (safe) | **TRUE directionally** but note: traditional leverage crept back toward 2.2x lease-adjusted / ~2.5x guided for YE-2026 as buybacks/capex consumed the cash. Net debt is a **liability to subtract** in any SoP, never "net cash." |
| Core customer resilient | Every call: "core customer... most consistent... never a down quarter since COVID" | Rev +4%, but destination/retail soft | **CREDIBLE** — corroborated by steady regional GGR; the weakness is in *destination/Strip*, which Boyd has low exposure to |
| Insider conviction | (not in transcript) | **0 open-market buys vs 24 sells (802k sh)** | **RED** — the real insider signal is net selling; the screener's buy_ratio 0.00 is the honest read |

**Net:** the operating business is a stable, cash-generative, slow-growth regional casino compounder. The narrative is **honest about operations** but the *screener-derived deep-value print is an accounting illusion* — strip the FanDuel gain and BYD trades ~18x FCF on +4% revenue growth and flat EBITDAR. That is **fairly-to-fully valued**, not 387% undervalued.

---

## 3. TONE & CONFIDENCE TRAJECTORY

**Rating: STABLE (high baseline, faint defensive drift).**

- **Q1-2025 — Confident-defensive:** "we remain confident in our ability to manage through the current environment" — pre-emptively defensive on macro/tariffs/Canada, but from strength.
- **Q2-2025 — Peak offensive:** "strongest [growth] in more than 3 years"; the FanDuel sale is delivered as a triumph. Most upbeat call.
- **Q3-2025 — Measured:** introduces "softer demand trends" on the Strip, "destination business" weakness; Hirsberg hedges hard on near-term trends ("3 weeks don't make a trend... we've been burned by trying to answer this"). Capital-allocation answer is deliberately non-committal ("we don't have an answer for you right now").
- **Q4-2025 — Confident on self-help, hopeful on macro:** leans on dated, controllable catalysts (Cadence, Suncoast, Ameristar) and an *external hope* (tax bill → consumer spending). "Nothing concerning, nothing I'm worried about."

The shift from offensive (growth/FanDuel) to a blend of *self-help + cost discipline + macro-hope* is mild but real. Hirsberg's COVID-era line — "there's really no concept of fixed costs beyond property taxes, insurance, rent and interest expense" (Q1) — is a tell that management is mentally rehearsing a downturn playbook even while reporting growth.

---

## 4. GUIDANCE CREDIBILITY

**Reliability: HIGH.** This is a management team that under-promises and delivers operationally.

- Property margins guided/described as "40%, consistent" — **delivered** every quarter (Locals >50%/47%, MSR >37%).
- FanDuel sale: announced Q2 ("close in next several weeks"), **closed July 31, 2025 exactly as promised**, proceeds used to delever exactly as stated. Clean execution.
- Online segment guidance was *revised down honestly* and early ($60M → $30-35M for 2026) rather than papered over — a credibility positive.
- Beat 6/7 quarters (86%) per the screener corroborates a modest-sandbagging pattern.
- The **one credibility caveat** is not about lying — it's that the *2026 macro tailwind* (tax-bill consumer boost) is an unprovable external bet they're leaning on, and destination-business softness was initially under-emphasized in Q1-Q2 before being acknowledged in Q3-Q4.

Score: **High** on operations/capital; **Medium** on the macro-dependent 2026 growth framing.

---

## 5. ANALYST PRESSURE POINTS

- **Strip/destination weakness (recurring, escalating):** Analysts (Beynon-Macquarie, Hargreaves-Barclays, Bender-Citizens) probed this every call from Q3 on. Management consistently *minimized and isolated* it ("less than one-tenth of 1%" Canada exposure; "it really is about the IP, not a broader issue"). This is the topic that went from non-issue (Q1) to a named ~$6M cash-hotel-revenue drag (Q4) — the analysts were right to push.
- **Capital allocation / what to do with FanDuel cash (Q3-Q4):** Repeatedly deflected — "we don't have an answer for you right now." The market wants a return-of-capital vs M&A answer; management is keeping optionality, which reads as *thoughtful* but leaves a re-rating catalyst undefined.
- **Margin flow-through (Politzer-JPM, Q2):** Pushed on why unrated play didn't lift margins; Hirsberg parried with weather/Easter. Fair, but a hint that incremental revenue isn't dropping through as richly as bulls hope.
- **Leverage target (Q4):** Hirsberg notably *would not* commit to a hard leverage floor — "probably just under three [lease-adjusted]... year-end estimate" — signaling leverage will *rise* again as cash is deployed.

---

## 6. RED FLAGS / GREEN FLAGS

🔴 **Screener fair-value is an artifact.** Buffett FV $420.8 / MoS +387% / Net margin 45% are all driven by the non-recurring FanDuel gain. EPV MoS is **−100%** and DCF MoS is **−1%** — the two methods that *don't* extrapolate the gain say the stock is **fully valued.** Methods disagree violently → low-confidence value signal.
🔴 **TTM FCF $281M, −30% YoY**, while P/FCF is 18.2x — organic cash generation is shrinking under the growth-capex load (Norfolk $750M, Suncoast, Cadence).
🔴 **Insider behavior:** 0 buys, 24 sells (802k shares) open-market. No conviction at $86.
🔴 **The catalyst is spent.** FanDuel is sold; the cash is largely returned/deployed; leverage is drifting back up. The re-rate already happened (stock at 78% of 52-wk range, +3% vs 200d).
🔴 **Online segment is structurally shrinking** ($63M → $30-35M) post-FanDuel rate reset.
🔴 **Destination/Strip softness** persisting into 2026 (Downtown pedestrian traffic −10%, IP weak ~6 months).

🟢 **Genuinely stable, defensive cash engine** — regional Locals business with core-customer resilience that "never had a down quarter since COVID."
🟢 **Best-in-class capital discipline** — 11% share count reduction in 2025, dividend raised to $0.20, clean FanDuel execution, balance sheet de-risked.
🟢 **Self-help growth pipeline is dated and controllable** (Cadence Q2-26, Suncoast Q3-26, Sky River expansion, Ameristar convention center) — not macro-dependent.
🟢 **Family-controlled (~30%), conservative** — low blow-up risk; but this *also caps* activist/takeover optionality.

---

## 7. HIDDEN SIGNALS

- **"There's really no concept of fixed costs"** (Hirsberg, Q1) — management is signaling a recession-defense mindset beneath the growth veneer. Reassuring on downside protection, but it's the language of a *late-cycle operator*, not a grower.
- **Market-access rates "adjusted"** in the FanDuel deal (Q2) — a quiet admission that the 2038 extension came at a *lower* recurring economics, which is exactly why the Online segment guide dropped by half. The headline $1.755B cash partly cannibalized a recurring royalty stream.
- **Leverage language drift:** "below 2x" (Q2) → "1.5x" (Q3) → "just under 3x lease-adjusted, year-end 2026" (Q4). The deleveraging is being *reversed* on purpose — the cash is being spent on buybacks, capex, and (eventually) M&A. The "fortress balance sheet" is a transient state.
- **M&A posture hardening:** "We're not afraid... to do larger transactions" (Q1-2026 per external reporting). Combined with the Penn Entertainment $9B speculation, Boyd is positioning as an **acquirer** — which is a *use of cash*, not a return of it, and introduces integration/overpayment risk rather than a clean re-rating catalyst.

---

## 8. CAPITAL-ALLOCATION VERDICT (for the director)

**Classification: STABLE** (with a spent special-situation overlay).

Boyd is a well-run, family-controlled regional casino operator throwing off steady cash, returning it aggressively, and executing a controllable self-help capex pipeline. The forensic record is clean — management does what it says. **But the deep-value thesis is an accounting mirage:** the +387% margin-of-safety and 45% net margin are manufactured by the one-time FanDuel gain. On recurring economics (≈18x P/FCF, +4% revenue, flat EBITDAR, shrinking TTM FCF, EPV MoS −100%, DCF MoS −1%), BYD is **fairly-to-fully valued, not deeply discounted.**

**The single most important thing the director must weigh before sizing:** *the load-bearing catalyst (FanDuel monetization) has already FIRED and been spent* — the cash is returned/deployed and leverage is climbing back. What's left is a quality compounder at a full price, with no dated, binding re-rating event, no activist (family bloc blocks it), and insiders net-selling. This is a **hold-quality business at a hold-quality price**, not an asymmetric special-situation.

**ECONOMIC MOAT:** Type = **regulatory-license + local scale-cost** (gaming licenses + entrenched Locals/Downtown-Hawaiian franchises). Width = **NARROW.** The evidence: ROIC **12.2%** (modestly above WACC, *not* fortress-level), gross margin **42.1% and contracting**, EBITDAR flat. The high *net* margin and ROE 42.6% are gain-inflated, not moat-derived. The license + family-relationship moat is real but bounded — regional gaming is competitive (Orleans losing share to Strip competition), capital-intensive, and demand-cyclical.

**MOAT_TREND: STABLE** (faintly eroding at the destination/Strip edge; core Locals stable). Returns are *not* rising and margins are softening → not widening; but the core regulatory-license base is durable → not collapsing.

**SECULAR THREAT: MANAGEABLE.** No terminal-value-destroying structural force. iGaming is the only candidate, and management frames it as *complementary* ("we see it as a new customer base") with credible evidence (PA/NJ experience). Regional land-based gaming is cyclically — not secularly — challenged. This is **not** a melting terminal base; cheapness (where it exists) would be a cyclical/macro fear-print, not a value trap. But there is *also no deep cheapness to exploit* once the FanDuel gain is stripped.

CREDIBILITY_SCORE: 4 | TRAJECTORY: STABLE | MOAT: NARROW | MOAT_TREND: STABLE | SECULAR_THREAT: manageable

Base case: ~$1.375B normalized total EBITDAR (Las Vegas Locals + Downtown + Midwest & South operating segments ~$1.2B at ~7.5x, plus Online ~$32M and Managed/Other ~$112M 2026 guide at ~9-10x for the fee-light businesses) x blended ~7.6x EV/EBITDAR = ~$10.45B EV. Less net debt $2.55B (LIABILITY, subtracted — NOT net cash; FanDuel proceeds already spent on delever+buybacks) = ~$7.9B equity / ~82M shares = ~$96 gross, trimmed to ~$92 for destination drag and re-levering. Reconciles bull $114 / bear $80. Sits just ABOVE the ~$85 price and the $85.6 DCF anchor = roughly fairly valued, modest single-digit upside, NOT a 397%-MoS bargain.
risk_reward: Roughly symmetric-to-slightly-favorable but NOT asymmetric. Downside-to-break ~$80 (bear SoP, ~5-6% below spot $84.68); upside-to-fair ~$92 base / ~$114 bull (~9% to ~35%). Net ~1.5:1 reward/risk skewed up only via the bull EBITDAR multiple and the mechanical ~10% buyback yield — there is no catalyst-driven re-rate. The buyback is the floor; destination softness + re-levering + depressed organic FCF cap the near-term ceiling.

At ~17.8x P/FCF and ROIC 12%, BYD is on the RICHER end of regional gaming vs CZR (~10x FCF) and PENN (~8x FCF) — i.e. NOT cheap relative to its own cohort. The peer comp explicitly confirms the multiple-method screen's 'value' read is driven by the FanDuel-inflated EPS (+264% 'likely from asset sales/tax items'), not a genuine discount, and concludes the valuation is FAIR. This is an INDEPENDENT confirmation that the deep-value flag is an artifact: BYD trades in line with / slightly rich to peers on normalized economics, with no idiosyncratic single-name discount to harvest.

# Boyd Gaming (BYD) — Interrogator Forensic Dossier

*Six consecutive quarterly calls, Q1-2025 through Q1-2026. A US regional casino operator (the screener ticker "BYD" = Boyd Gaming, NOT the Chinese EV maker). Management = Keith Smith (CEO) / Josh Hirsberg (CFO), the same two voices throughout — a stable, long-tenured team.*

## 1. NARRATIVE ARC

The story is remarkably **consistent and low-drama** across all six quarters — which is itself the signal. The spine never changes: a diversified regional-casino portfolio (Las Vegas Locals, Downtown/Hawaiian, Midwest & South, Online, Managed) where the **"core customer" is the load-bearing pillar** ("they're core to our business and they've been very consistent," Q1-25; "we've not seen a quarter where that customer has been down" — *since COVID*). Every call repeats the same triad: core-customer strength + operating discipline (40% property margins "since 2021") + a $100M/yr growth-capital program (Cadence Crossing, Norfolk VA $750M, Ameristar St. Charles convention center, Par-A-Dice rebuild, Suncoast modernization).

The **one genuine narrative event** is the FanDuel monetization. Q1-25 frames the 5% FanDuel stake as embedded optionality ("significant and growing value for our shareholders"). Q2-25 converts it: the **$1.755B cash sale to Flutter** announced two weeks before the call, market-access extended to 2038. By Q3-25/Q4-25 the proceeds are spent — leverage taken "below 2x," then to **1.5x** (Q3-25), funding **$800M+ of buybacks that cut the share count 11% in 2025**. By Q1-26 the narrative has fully pivoted from "unlock FanDuel value" to "**deploy the firepower**" — M&A optionality ("not afraid... to do larger transactions") plus a standing ~$650M/yr return program (~$9/share).

A **defensive sub-plot emerges and grows** from Q3-25 onward: **Las Vegas destination/Strip softness** bleeding into the Orleans and Downtown (Fremont Street pedestrian traffic −10% Q4-25, **−11% Q1-26**). Management quarantines it surgically ("think about it as the IP, not a broader issue"; "narrow... to just the Orleans"). The offsetting growth driver quietly introduced is **"customers staying closer to home"** — a recession-hedge framing for the Midwest & South segment, which becomes the portfolio's workhorse (strongest Q3 in 3 years; +4% rev / +5% EBITDAR Q1-26).

Quietly **dropped**: the FanDuel equity narrative (gone post-sale, replaced by a *lower* online segment guide — $63M 2025 → **$30-35M 2026**, because the sale traded the equity stake for adjusted market-access rates). Portfolio **pruning** introduced: Sam's Town Tunica closed (Nov-25), Sam's Town Shreveport sold to Bally's (Feb-26).

## 2. CLAIMS vs FINANCIALS (forensic)

The single most important forensic finding: **the screener's headline valuation metrics are contaminated by the one-time FanDuel gain and must be discarded.**

| Management claim | Financial evidence | Verdict |
|---|---|---|
| "Record company-wide revenues" / steady growth | Rev 3.74B→3.93B→4.09B (FY23→24→25); **+4.1% YoY, +4.8% 3yr CAGR** | **MATCHES** — low-single-digit, exactly "mature regional casino" |
| "EBITDAR ~$1.4B, property margins 40% consistent since 2021" | Corroborated by the quarterly EBITDAR prints ($338/358/322/337/317M) | **MATCHES** — genuinely stable |
| EPS "+264.5% YoY" to 22.56 (screener headline) | Driven by the **$1.755B FanDuel sale gain**, NOT operations | **DIVERGES — DO NOT TRUST.** "Net margin 45%" and "Buffett fair value 420.8 / MoS 397%" are **artifacts of a non-recurring asset-sale gain.** Underlying NI was ~578-640M in 2022-24; the 1.84B 2025 print is inflated by FanDuel. The DCF fair value **85.6 ≈ price 84.68** is the honest anchor. |
| "Robust free cash flow... returned $800M+" | But **TTM FCF = 281M** (FMP, latest-Q, "use over annual"); FCF −30% YoY, 3yr CAGR −18% | **TENSION.** The $800M of returns in 2025 was funded by the **FanDuel cash**, not organic FCF. Organic FCF is being consumed by the elevated build-out (Norfolk $750M, Suncoast, Cadence). The buyback is sustainable only if EBITDAR holds AND capex normalizes post-2027. |
| "Strongest balance sheet in our history," leverage 1.5x (Q3-25) | Net debt **2.55B** recomputed (total debt 2.92B − cash 373M); leverage guided **back up toward 2.5x** by YE-2026 | **HONEST but RE-LEVERING.** The 1.5x trough was transient; capex ($650-700M 2026) + buybacks push it back to ~2.5x traditional / ~3x lease-adjusted. ROIC 12.2% > WACC but not a fortress return. |
| Core customer "never down since COVID" | Can't be disproven from segment data; destination weakness is real and admitted | **CREDIBLE** — they distinguish core vs destination honestly rather than hiding the Strip drag. |

**Bottom line:** the narrative is **HONEST and the numbers back the *operating* story** (stable ~$1.4B EBITDAR, 40% margins, +4% rev). But the screener's *deep-value* flag (397% MoS, 45% net margin, EPS +264%) is a **data artifact of the FanDuel gain** — the business is roughly fairly valued at ~17.8x P/FCF on the DCF, not a 4-bagger.

## 3. TONE & CONFIDENCE TRAJECTORY

**STABLE-to-mildly-defensive.** This is a management team that has run the same playbook for years and speaks with measured, almost monotone confidence — no euphoria, no panic.

- **Q1-25 (cautious-confident):** "we have not seen any meaningful shift in consumer behavior... we recognize that the last several weeks have brought an increased level of economic uncertainty." Tariff/macro hedging language present but managed.
- **Q2-25 (most offensive):** FanDuel close = victory lap. "unlocking the significant value." "property-level... growth was the strongest in more than 3 years." Confidence peak.
- **Q3-25 (balanced):** introduces destination weakness candidly ("softness in destination business... primarily at the Orleans") but frames it as contained.
- **Q4-25 / Q1-26 (defensive-tilted):** more time spent explaining what went *wrong* — weather ($5M), Tunica closure ($4M), Suncoast disruption ($1.5M→$3M), destination ($5-6M). Language shifts toward **"operational discipline," "efficiencies," "staying closer to home"** — classic defense-mode vocabulary. The $45-50M "tax benefit" (accelerated depreciation, a *timing* difference Hirsberg carefully clarifies is "not a refund") is volunteered as a cash cushion.

The team is **MORE specific, not less**, over time — they quantify every headwind ($5M weather, $1.5M Suncoast, $6M hotel revenue) and even walk analysts to a do-it-yourself model ("we've given you enough information to figure it out"). That specificity is a credibility positive.

## 4. GUIDANCE CREDIBILITY — **HIGH**

- **FanDuel:** promised "close and receive proceeds in the next several weeks" (Q2-25) → delivered, leverage <2x then 1.5x by Q3-25. **Delivered exactly.**
- **Online segment:** *raised* mid-year ($60M Q3-25) then printed $63M FY25 — **slight over-delivery**, then honestly guided it *down* to $30-35M for 2026 (the FanDuel rate reset). No sandbagging games; they tell you the bad guide.
- **Suncoast disruption:** Q3-25 they downplayed ("performing on par"); Q1-26 they came clean ("we said we'll let you know when we see it... we're seeing it"). They **under-promised then admitted the hit** — honest, if it means the prior optimism was slightly off.
- Screener shows **beat 6/7 qtrs (86%)** — consistent with a team that guides conservatively and delivers.

**Reliability: HIGH.** No pattern of broken promises; headwinds are pre-announced and quantified; capital-allocation commitments (delever, then return ~$650M/yr) have been honored.

## 5. ANALYST PRESSURE POINTS

1. **Las Vegas destination/Strip softness** — the dominant recurring worry (Beynon, Hargreaves, Politzer, Hardiman across Q1-25→Q1-26). Management consistently *narrows* it to the Orleans + IP Biloxi and insists locals/core are insulated. This is the topic most likely to be "later turned into bad news" — it has steadily worsened (Fremont −10%→−11%), and the Q1-26 question "is it still possible locals can eke out growth?" got a non-committal "we've given you enough to figure it out" — i.e., **probably not**.
2. **What to do with the $1.5x-leverage firepower / M&A** — pressed every call post-FanDuel. Management deflects with "thoughtful, long-term view... could be M&A, could be other things, could tick back up." No commitment = optionality, but also no catalyst.
3. **Promotional intensity / margin sustainability in a downturn** — Hirsberg's "no concept of fixed costs beyond property taxes, insurance, rent and interest" (Q1-25) is a reassuring but *defensive* answer that pre-positions for revenue softness.
4. **iGaming/sports-betting cannibalization risk** (Hawaii bill, Virginia) — deflected as "complementary, not detrimental," which is the self-interested read.

## 6. RED FLAGS / GREEN FLAGS

🔴 **Destination/Strip business in structural-looking decline** — Fremont pedestrian traffic −11% (Q1-26), hotel revenue −$6M, two-plus years of Orleans weakness. Not yet bleeding into locals, but the gap is widening.
🔴 **Organic FCF collapsed to $281M TTM** (−30% YoY) — the $800M of 2025 returns was FanDuel cash, not earnings. Buyback math gets tighter once the one-time cash is gone and capex stays high through 2027.
🔴 **Re-levering back to ~2.5x/~3x lease-adj.** by YE-2026 — the "fortress balance sheet" was a momentary trough.
🔴 **Heavy capex cycle** ($650-700M 2026; Norfolk $750M, Par-A-Dice, Suncoast, Cadence) with returns not landing until 2027-28.
🔴 **Screener valuation is corrupted** — the 397% MoS is a FanDuel-gain artifact; the honest DCF says fairly valued.

🟢 **Genuinely stable operating base** — ~$1.4B EBITDAR, 40% property margins held "since 2021," core customer never down since COVID.
🟢 **Disciplined, shareholder-friendly capital allocation** — 11% share-count reduction in 2025, ~$9/share of buyback+dividend ongoing, $700M authorization.
🟢 **Honest, specific, long-tenured management** — quantifies every headwind, no sandbagging, delivered the FanDuel monetization cleanly.
🟢 **Active portfolio pruning** (Tunica, Shreveport→Bally's) and prudent M&A discipline ("sellers want a part of the synergies... we walk").
🟢 **High-return organic projects** (Treasure Chest "after-tax returns probably over 25%").

## 7. HIDDEN SIGNALS

- **"Staying closer to home"** — introduced Q3-25, repeated every call since. This is management *pre-framing a consumer slowdown as a tailwind* for the regional/Midwest book. It's a tell that they expect the macro to soften and are positioning the narrative defensively.
- **The online guide-down ($63M→$30-35M)** is buried as a "revenue-share change" — it's the real cost of the FanDuel sale (they gave up upside for cash + a 2038 access deal). Honestly disclosed but easy to miss.
- **M&A posture flipped from buyer-of-synergies to disciplined-walker** — "sellers now have a very high expectation of getting a part of those synergies... so that's the bigger dynamic." Management is signaling **deals are expensive and they won't overpay** — good discipline, but it caps the M&A catalyst.
- **The $45-50M "tax benefit" is a timing item, not income** — Hirsberg pre-empts any analyst tempted to model it as recurring. Careful, credible framing.
- **Caesars/Fertitta chatter** (external, post-transcript): BYD is cast as a *beneficiary/acquirer of divested assets*, not a target — consistent with management's "firepower" posture.

## 8. CAPITAL-ALLOCATION VERDICT (for the director)

**Classification: STABLE** (with a defensive tilt). This is a well-run, fairly-valued, mature regional-casino cash compounder — not a deep-value mispricing and not a melting asset. The screener's "deep_value / 397% MoS" tag is a **false positive driven by the one-time FanDuel gain**; strip it out and BYD trades at ~17.8x P/FCF on a DCF fair value (~$85.6) essentially equal to the price.

**The single most important thing the director must weigh before sizing:** the entire "value" signal is a **FanDuel-gain artifact** — there is no genuine sum-of-parts discount or catalyst left (FanDuel is FIRED/spent, the asset sales are tiny, M&A is optional and management is a disciplined walk-away buyer). What you are actually buying is a steady ~$1.4B-EBITDAR operator returning ~$9/share, into a **softening Las Vegas destination backdrop and a re-levering, heavy-capex window through 2027**, where organic FCF has fallen to $281M TTM. The buyback is the only "catalyst," and it is a slow, mechanical re-rate lever, not an event.

**MOAT:** **NARROW.** Boyd's edge is a **regulatory-license + local-density** moat (limited Nevada/regional gaming licenses, entrenched locals customer relationships, 50-year Hawaiian brand). It is *real but not wide*: ROIC is 12.2% (above WACC but not exceptional), gross margin is **42.1% and explicitly "contracting,"** and the destination segment is structurally soft. This is a moat that protects the *locals/core* franchise well but does **not** confer pricing power on the destination/Strip-exposed assets. **MOAT_TREND: STABLE** — returns are holding, not widening, with margin pressure at the edges (destination) offset by locals strength and efficiency.

**SECULAR THREAT:** **MANAGEABLE / none-terminal.** Regional gaming faces real but *gradual* pressures — iGaming/sports-betting cannibalization (management argues complementary, and the data so far supports a slow, not catastrophic, shift) and Las Vegas tourism cyclicality. Neither is a terminal-value-destroying secular force on the *locals/regional* base, which is demographically supported (Southern Nevada population +16%/decade, wage growth +6%). The cheapness here is **not** a value trap on a shrinking base — but it is **also not cheapness at all** once the FanDuel artifact is removed. This is a *fairly-valued stable compounder*, so the "value" thesis fails on **lack of a discount**, not on terminal erosion.

CREDIBILITY_SCORE: 4 | TRAJECTORY: STABLE | MOAT: NARROW | MOAT_TREND: STABLE | SECULAR_THREAT: manageable
